finding: Associated with: obstructing biliary stone or malignancy

differentials.name: biliary sepsis, CNS depression, lethargy, mental confusion, shock
differentials.raw: biliary sepsis, CNS depression, lethargy, mental confusion, shock (50%)

differentials.name: recurrent episodes of sepsis + RUQ pain
differentials.raw: recurrent episodes of sepsis + RUQ pain

differentials.name: bile cultures in 90% positive for infection
differentials.raw: bile cultures in 90% positive for infection

differentials.name: Reynolds pentad = fever, pain, jaundice, shock, lethargy
differentials.raw: Reynolds pentad = fever, pain, jaundice, shock, lethargy

differentials.name: opportunistic organism isolated from bile (in 50%) (b)  direct invasion of epithelium: Herpes simplex virus
differentials.raw: opportunistic organism isolated from bile (in 50%) (b)  direct invasion of epithelium: Herpes simplex virus

differentials.name: abnormal LFTs (esp. serum alkaline phosphatase; normal / mildly elevated bilirubin);
differentials.raw: abnormal LFTs (esp. serum alkaline phosphatase; normal / mildly elevated bilirubin);

differentials.name: combination of sclerosing cholangitis appearance + papillary stenosis
differentials.raw: combination of sclerosing cholangitis appearance + papillary stenosis (50%)

differentials.name: long-segment extrahepatic duct stricture in isolation / with intrahepatic disease
differentials.raw: long-segment extrahepatic duct stricture in isolation / with intrahepatic disease (15%)

differentials.name: Primary sclerosing cholangitis
differentials.raw: Primary sclerosing cholangitis (multifocal + short intrahepatic biliary strictures with beaded / "pruned-tree" lesions)

differentials.name: Cholangiocarcinoma
differentials.raw: Cholangiocarcinoma (confined to bile ducts, not affecting pancreatic duct, hepatic capsular retraction)

differentials.name: Inflammatory bowel disease (ulcerative colitis in 50-74%, Crohn disease in 13%) ◊  1-4% of patients with inflammatory bowel disease develop primary sclerosing cholangitis!
differentials.raw: Inflammatory bowel disease (ulcerative colitis in 50-74%, Crohn disease in 13%) ◊  1-4% of patients with inflammatory bowel disease develop primary sclerosing cholangitis!

differentials.name: Cirrhosis, chronic active hepatitis, pericholangitis, fatty degeneration
differentials.raw: Cirrhosis, chronic active hepatitis, pericholangitis, fatty degeneration

differentials.name: abnormal liver function tests: serum bilirubin, serum alkaline phosphatase, γ- glutamyltransferase
differentials.raw: abnormal liver function tests: serum bilirubin, serum alkaline phosphatase, γ- glutamyltransferase

differentials.name: fever, night sweats, chills, RUQ pain, pruritus
differentials.raw: fever, night sweats, chills, RUQ pain, pruritus (10-15%)

differentials.name: ANCA and antinuclear antibodies
differentials.raw: ANCA and antinuclear antibodies(low titers with low specificity)

differentials.name: history of previous biliary surgery
differentials.raw: history of previous biliary surgery (53%) + chronic / recurrent pancreatitis (14%)

differentials.name: Extrahepatic ducts only (2-3%) Dx:   PSC is diagnosed in patients with clinical + biochemical signs of cholestasis, typical cholangiographic imaging findings and after exclusion of secondary causes of sclerosing cholangitis.
differentials.raw: Extrahepatic ducts only (2-3%) Dx:   PSC is diagnosed in patients with clinical + biochemical signs of cholestasis, typical cholangiographic imaging findings and after exclusion of secondary causes of sclerosing cholangitis.

differentials.name: screening with tumor marker CA-19.9 > 129 U/mL (79% sensitive, 99% specific) (b)  colorectal cancer (10- to 14-fold increased risk compared to general population) (c)  pancreatic cancer
differentials.raw: screening with tumor marker CA-19.9 > 129 U/mL (79% sensitive, 99% specific) (b)  colorectal cancer (10- to 14-fold increased risk compared to general population) (c)  pancreatic cancer

differentials.name: Palliative: ursodeoxycholic acid, dilatation of dominant strictures
differentials.raw: Palliative: ursodeoxycholic acid, dilatation of dominant strictures

differentials.name: IgG4-related sclerosing cholangitis Primary sclerosing cholangitis occurs in younger patients, is associated with inflammatory bowel disease, and is less acute with longer duration of symptoms compared to IgG4-related sclerosing cholangitis.
differentials.raw: IgG4-related sclerosing cholangitis Primary sclerosing cholangitis occurs in younger patients, is associated with inflammatory bowel disease, and is less acute with longer duration of symptoms compared to IgG4-related sclerosing cholangitis.

differentials.name: Sclerosing cholangiocarcinoma
differentials.raw: Sclerosing cholangiocarcinoma (progressive cholangiographic changes within 0.5-1.5 years of initial diagnosis, marked ductal dilatation upstream from a dominant stricture, intraductal mass > 1 cm in diameter)

differentials.name: Primary biliary cirrhosis
differentials.raw: Primary biliary cirrhosis (disease limited to intrahepatic ducts, strictures less pronounced, pruning + crowding of bile ducts, normal AMA titer)

differentials.name: poor nutritional + low socioeconomic status of patient
differentials.raw: poor nutritional + low socioeconomic status of patient

differentials.name: recurrent attacks of fever, chills, abdominal pain, jaundice Location:  particularly in lateral segment of L lobe + posterior segment of R lobe + extrahepatic duct
differentials.raw: recurrent attacks of fever, chills, abdominal pain, jaundice Location:  particularly in lateral segment of L lobe + posterior segment of R lobe + extrahepatic duct

differentials.name: Caroli disease
differentials.raw: Caroli disease (saccular dilatation of intrahepatic bile ducts)

differentials.name: Primary sclerosing cholangitis
differentials.raw: Primary sclerosing cholangitis (focal discontinuous bile duct dilatation)

differentials.name: Clonorchiasis
differentials.raw: Clonorchiasis (biliary ductal dilatation limited to intrahepatic bile ducts)

differentials.name: Intraductal stone disease / choledocholithiasis → bile duct stricture → recurrent pyogenic cholangitis
differentials.raw: Intraductal stone disease / choledocholithiasis → bile duct stricture → recurrent pyogenic cholangitis

differentials.name: Ischemic cholangiopathy from treatment with intraarterial chemotherapy / floxuridine
differentials.raw: Ischemic cholangiopathy from treatment with intraarterial chemotherapy / floxuridine

differentials.name: Previous biliary tract surgery / blunt trauma
differentials.raw: Previous biliary tract surgery / blunt trauma

context: √  purulent material fills biliary ducts √  marked inhomogeneous hepatic parenchymal enhancement during arterial phase (60%) Prognosis:  100% mortality if not decompressed; 40-60% mortality with treatment; 13-16% overall mortality rate Organism:  E coli (31%), Klebsiella pneumoniae (17%), Enterococcus faecalis (17%), Streptococcus (17%) √  may have gas in biliary tree CECT: √  nodular / patchy / wedge-shaped / geographic inhomogeneous transient hepatic parenchymal enhancement in periportal location on hepatic arterial phase (= hyperemic changes around bile ducts) √  diffuse concentric thickeni
